title: melicope obovata
intro: Melicope obovata, also known as the Australian yellow myrtle, is a small tree or shrub native to the eastern coast of Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and limonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Melicope obovata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for medicinal use, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
